LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, DEFENDANT GEFEN GALLERY, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED FROM MAR-15-2022 TO APR-12-2022 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPT. 302 PER THE AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES (PER EMAIL DATED MAR-09-2022). (302/RBU)

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, APR-12-2022 HEARING HELD RE: DEFENDANT GEFEN GALLERY, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT'S "DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT" IS OVERRULED ON EACH OF TWO INDEPENDENT GROUNDS. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS.) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE COURT'S RULING AND COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-12-2022 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ROBERT RICH, Esq. of Belzer & Murray, LLP (Tel: 925-284-9000) for defendant Gefen Gallery, LLC;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT GEFEN GALLERY, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's "demurrer to complaint" is overruled on each of two independent grounds.

First, the demurrer fails to adequately state its grounds, employing a statutory label instead of asserting any specific issues. (See CRC 3.1320.)

Second, the complaint pleads the elements of its two causes of action. (Id. at 6:13-7:19; CACI 3061.) That suffices. Defendant says "in California there is a requirement to plead that the limitation on the access to web content has an impact on the use of physical facilities." (Memo. 2:7-9.) However, defendant cites no authority for this heightened pleading requirement. Indeed, two of the three cases in defendant's brief are from federal courts, which do not use California demurrer rules. The other case is in the summary judgment context, not at the pleading stage.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the Court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
